title: Oreogrammitis universa
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis universa is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Oreogrammitis, which is a group of small to medium-sized ferns. The species is native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands. Oreogrammitis universa is also known to be an epiphyte, growing on other plants for support. Its classification further places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta.

intro: Oreogrammitis universa, also known as the "Universal Fern," is a species of fern native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Oreogrammitis universa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oreogrammitis universa is commonly prepared as a tea or a tincture to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and flu, as well as to promote overall well-being.
